Egypt brokered the deal after months of rocket attacks from Gaza and Israeli military responses.
1c0fbe7e6758334d83d145580688e7aa1c549c53:
Barack Obama proposed billions in spending for education, infrastructure, energy, and health care to boost the economy.
He plans to fund these initiatives by ending the Iraq war, cutting waste, taxing polluters, and ending Bush-era tax cuts.
Obama rejected protectionist trade policies, advocating for global competitiveness and embracing globalization.
John McCain's campaign criticized Obama's plan, arguing it would slow growth and raise taxes.
820c917dada8b32be365eaeec9edc1ca5833cfcd:
Slovakian referee Lubos Michel will officiate the Champions League final between Manchester United and Chelsea in Moscow.
Michel is experienced, having refereed major tournaments including Euro 2004 and the 2006 World Cup.
Manchester police are in Moscow to advise Russian authorities and prevent fan violence during the final.
The British embassy in Moscow will extend hours and offer a hotline to assist English fans attending the match.
faf3821150b0c63290cbbddd7a8b54900995ea5a:
Kimi Raikkonen criticized Lewis Hamilton for causing a pit-lane crash at the Canadian Grand Prix.
Hamilton's mistake ended both drivers' races and cost Raikkonen a potential win.
Ferrari's team director called Hamilton's error "serious" and supported the FIA's 10-place grid penalty.
Hamilton apologized, calling the incident unfortunate and not intentional.
1de0cae2832cf41f49e6fad8254247c789dfabd2:
UK Foreign Office warns of increased terrorist threat in the United Arab Emirates.
Britons urged to be vigilant, especially in public places frequented by expatriates and travelers.
Over one million Britons visited the UAE in 2006; more than 100,000 live there.
No similar warning issued by the U.S. State Department as of Monday.
6c6e51ef20def117ed9114ed8d8480b3a100f1c3:
Osama bin Laden plans to release a new message addressing Western nations, according to al Qaeda's media wing.
The message will mark the 60th anniversary of the Israeli occupation.
It is unclear if the message will be audio or video, or when it will be released.
Bin Laden's previous messages criticized Western involvement in Iraq and Afghanistan and the publication of controversial cartoons.
b8c755b8921f7917d161605d2ef404d9551f3846:
Rising Mississippi River floodwaters threaten multiple Illinois and Missouri towns, prompting emergency levee construction.
Over 1,100 National Guard members and inmates assist with sandbagging and flood defense efforts.
Hundreds evacuated, major bridges closed, and power shut off in some communities as levees are breached.
Iowa begins recovery as floodwaters recede, but damage to crops, infrastructure, and businesses is extensive.
dc7e18d14b8ad29abbd79f554324ec7b8ba8b9c0:
Security expert Gavin de Becker emphasizes the importance of trusting your intuition to prevent danger.
Real-life stories show ignoring gut feelings can lead to serious harm, while listening can avert crime.
De Becker argues that fear is a protective instinct, not a weakness, and should be heeded.
He urges people, especially women, to recognize that uneasy feelings are valid warning signs.
a7fc90db6c296663b1b7fc057451dfd45019342d:
A woman discovered her stillborn son's body was kept in a hospital jar for four years.
She believed he had been cremated and repeatedly requested funeral papers and ashes.
The mother is now suing the hospital for medical negligence and emotional distress.
The hospital has apologized for the error and confirmed legal proceedings are underway.
7f22fbc4fd81e50c632c5853856965aa4d0065d4:
Andre Berto won the vacant WBC welterweight title by stopping Miguel Rodriguez in the seventh round.
Mikkel Kessler became WBA supermiddle champion after knocking out Dimitri Sartison in Copenhagen.
Amir Khan defended his Commonwealth lightweight title, overcoming a knockdown to stop Michael Gomez in the fifth round.
Khan described the fight as one of his toughest and vowed to learn from his mistakes.
309ae632df7b88a9569d81fa91aff3b9fb03529e:
Michelle Obama is refining her public message for the general election, focusing on personal stories and family background.
She faces criticism for being perceived as too negative or angry and is working to connect more personally with voters.
Her campaign denies an "image makeover," emphasizing a strategy to introduce her to a broader audience.
Michelle Obama remains independent, choosing her own style and approach as she defines her role in the spotlight.
732304a0901ff14d023d4361c1d41a85724808f3:
Zico is leaving Fenerbahce after failing to agree on a new contract.

Fenerbahce may replace him with Spain coach Luis Aragones.

Dozens of 911 calls reported panic and gunfire during the Omaha mall shooting.

Police say Hawkins had mental health issues and no clear motive for targeting the store.

Australian cricketers will honor Jane McGrath during a match against West Indies by wearing pink ribbons.
Jane McGrath, wife of Glenn McGrath, died at 42 after a long battle with cancer.
The pink color represents the McGrath Foundation, supporting breast cancer awareness and fundraising.
Jane and Glenn McGrath were recognized for their charity work with the Order of Australia.
6db7e70883f0241081833487c421bab86fdac26e:
Gabe Watson has been charged in the 2003 death of his wife during a honeymoon scuba dive in Australia.
An Australian coroner found inconsistencies in Watson's statements and ruled there was enough evidence for trial.

The couple had been married just 11 days before the incident on the Great Barrier Reef.
e51e06234b3f49da16fec69aaee6fb9cd09cb1d8:
Nepalese authorities detained at least 650 Tibetan exiles protesting China's policies ahead of the Olympic torch relay.
Three Tibetan community leaders were arrested and charged for organizing the protests.

The crackdown comes just days before the Olympic torch relay in Tibet's capital, Lhasa.
42474528588bac3a27842671a3fa940fed450552:
Betty Neumar, 76, is charged with soliciting the murder of her husband Harold Gentry in 1986.
Authorities are investigating the deaths of her four other husbands, but she faces charges only in North Carolina.

Family members describe Neumar as becoming distant and secretive after marrying their relatives.
5932d9c8f1da55bad396197ebae0853c515180a5:
The Supreme Court ruled 5-4 that child rapists cannot be given the death penalty, reserving it for murderers only.
Justice Kennedy cited "evolving standards of decency" and a national consensus against capital punishment for non-murder crimes.
The decision affects laws in six states that allowed the death penalty for child rape, aligning with broader restrictions on capital punishment.
The ruling also considered the risk of incentivizing murder of victims and the burden placed on child witnesses.
daacc676fc659bfd147d11b1409ab42615a2f169:
Female suicide bombers in Iraq are increasing, with 20 attacks in the first half of 2008.
The "Daughters of Iraq" program trains women to search females at checkpoints, countering this threat.

Cultural norms make this work a social revolution in conservative rural communities.
7a02e707b139791920fb13115d6132541fff7d97:
Investigators are focusing on farms in Mexico and Florida as possible sources of a salmonella outbreak linked to tomatoes.
Over 500 people in 32 states and D.C. have contracted the same strain of salmonella since April.

At least 53 people have been hospitalized, and one death may be linked to the outbreak.
db1972f0204c7a9c292f4c85e8838251cb26625e:
Congress overrode President Bush's veto of a $300 billion farm bill for the second time.
A clerical error required Congress to re-pass the bill after 34 pages were missing in the original.

Bush objected to subsidies for the wealthy and claimed the bill increased spending using budget gimmicks.
6defcdfd6995661fd681dc15163d67f6a5d6727b:
Luke Russert discussed youth voting, his father's legacy, and his own future in journalism on "Larry King Live."
He shared how political discussions were a regular part of his upbringing with Tim Russert.
Luke reflected on learning about his father's death while abroad and the private time it allowed for grieving.
He expressed interest in continuing political commentary and encouraging youth involvement in politics.
24716f5a39d23dafbe9a9df8fa002788d14d020b:
NASA's Mars Phoenix Lander successfully touched down near Mars' north pole, capturing images of the surface.
The lander's 90-day mission will analyze Martian soil and permafrost for signs of past or present life.
Phoenix uses a robotic arm to scoop ice and dirt, searching for organic material preserved in the ice.
The mission overcame past failures and engineering challenges, reviving a previously shelved spacecraft design.
ab3c98cf7833b842898a568d872e45ceb2f7dda9:
Pregnant soldier Spc. Megan Lynn Touma was found dead in a Fayetteville, NC motel room.
Touma, 23, was assigned to Fort Bragg and was seven months pregnant.
Her body was discovered after police responded to reports of a strong odor.
She is the second pregnant service member to die in North Carolina in recent months.
831005755f85012c882f17c3b3699b34a7febb7b:
Hamas' takeover of Gaza brought relative safety but severe shortages of food, gas, and basic goods.
Israel's blockade has crippled Gaza's economy, leaving most residents dependent on UN food aid.
Daily life is a struggle, with long waits for fuel and basic supplies, and widespread unemployment.
Many Gazans blame both Hamas and Fatah for their hardships, expressing frustration and despair.
0590a8c653b7d332d9df195dcf3ce014abb4eff7:
Opposition supporters in Zimbabwe report severe torture and violence ahead of the presidential runoff.
Morgan Tsvangirai withdrew from the race, citing impossible conditions for a fair election.

Mugabe's government denies violence, calling the vote "free and fair" despite international criticism.
f79341438eed40986c3219b67a3b5d87eb6c018a:
Four opposition activists and the wife of Harare's mayor were killed ahead of Zimbabwe's presidential runoff.
U.S. Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice accused President Mugabe of sponsoring violence against opposition supporters.

Zimbabwe faces escalating violence, political intimidation, and economic collapse as the election approaches.
8db6225f67580d64a196f2f3197386a6be950a09:

Obama gained four more delegates, maintaining his overall lead in delegates and states won.

Party unity remains uncertain, as many Clinton supporters say they may not back Obama in the general election.
e386d9ed70bac668fa6048b572f445d9b7b62f75:
North Korea submitted a nuclear declaration and began dismantling its Yongbyon reactor, prompting some U.S. sanctions to be lifted.  
Republican lawmakers criticized the deal as incomplete, citing lack of details on weapons and suspected proliferation to Syria.

One Texas A&M mariner was found dead after their sailboat capsized in the Gulf of Mexico.
Five other crewmates were rescued after surviving hours in the water with only four life jackets.

The missing keel caused rapid flooding, leading to the capsizing and loss of communication.
d3ca193f45aef9aa766d52fbc58d6b8f1658ee60:
Hillary Clinton returns to the Senate after ending her presidential campaign, receiving a warm welcome from colleagues.
She pledges to support Barack Obama and campaign for Democratic Senate candidates in the upcoming election.
Clinton emphasizes the need for Democratic unity and legislative progress on health care, the economy, and Iraq.
Both Obama and McCain acknowledge Clinton's increased influence and leadership following her historic primary run.
1ca9bf09bf534add02b7cfe6ebae6d17cde66286:
Prince William helped the U.S. Coast Guard intercept a speedboat carrying nearly a ton of cocaine.
The cocaine, valued at $80 million, was found in the Atlantic Ocean northeast of Barbados.

Senate Republicans blocked a Democratic bill to tax oil companies' windfall profits amid record energy prices.
The measure failed to reach the 60 votes needed, with a 51-43 vote; six Republicans supported it.

Republicans called the proposal a political "gimmick" that would hurt consumers and not solve high gas prices.

Five European divers stranded on an Indonesian island fought off a Komodo dragon with rocks.
They survived two nights by scavenging for shellfish before being rescued by rangers.
The group was dehydrated but in good condition and taken to a hospital for examination.
Strong tides are being investigated as a possible cause of their disappearance.

The Bush administration has escalated covert operations in Iran, targeting nuclear sites and destabilizing the government.
Congress reportedly authorized up to $400 million for these secret missions involving U.S. commandos and Iranian dissidents.

Tensions rise as Israel conducts military exercises simulating a potential strike on Iranian nuclear facilities.

President Bush signed a $162 billion bill funding the Iraq and Afghanistan wars through spring 2009.

Bush and White House officials called the bill a bipartisan victory supporting U.S. troops.

Barack Obama defended his patriotism in a speech, citing his deep love for America.
He addressed criticisms about his flag pin, the Pledge of Allegiance, and his wife's comments.

He praised John McCain's military service and distanced himself from Gen. Wesley Clark's remarks.

Bangladesh police have detained over 18,000 people in an 11-day crackdown ahead of December elections.
Human rights groups and opposition parties allege the arrests are politically motivated to sideline rivals.

About 220 Zimbabweans sought refuge outside the U.S. Embassy in Harare due to election-related violence.

Sheley, a meth addict with a violent history, faces first-degree murder and other charges; bail set at $1 million.

Authorities believe Sheley had ties to some victims and acted randomly in other cases; investigation continues.

A Paris court ordered eBay to pay $63 million to LVMH for allowing counterfeit goods sales.
LVMH argued eBay's negligence hurt its luxury brands by enabling fake products on the site.
eBay claims it removes counterfeits quickly and will appeal, calling the ruling anti-competitive.

About 50,000 South Koreans protested in Seoul against the government's decision to import U.S. beef.
Protests began after the government lifted a five-year ban imposed due to mad cow disease concerns.

The protests have led to government reshuffling and delayed a planned visit by U.S. President George W. Bush.

Will Smith led over 46,000 people in singing "Happy Birthday" to Nelson Mandela at a London concert.

Stars like Queen, Amy Winehouse, and Quincy Jones participated, with global leaders attending.

A mining company may have found the largest gold deposit ever in the British Isles near Clontibret, Ireland.

Analysts caution the estimate is preliminary and actual value depends on gold quality and concentration.

A U.S. soldier used a Quran for target practice, sparking outrage among Iraqi leaders and citizens.
The Iraqi Islamic Party demands the "severest of punishments," not just an apology and reassignment.

Protests and condemnation followed, with calls for accountability and respect for Islamic sanctities.

President Cristina Fernandez defended increased grain export taxes, urging farmers to respect the law during protests.
She announced Congress will debate the tax hike, though her party controls both houses.

Fernandez called for public support and peaceful dialogue, discouraging road blockades and disruptions.

Three American contractors rescued after five years in Colombian jungle captivity are in good health, doctors report.

FARC still holds over 700 hostages in Colombia's jungles despite the successful rescue operation.

About 6 million Chinese-made tire valve stems are being recalled by an Ohio distributor.
Only about 8,600 of the 6 million valves are estimated to be defective.
Defective valves may crack after six months, causing gradual tire pressure loss and possible accidents.
The defect is linked to improper rubber mixing at the manufacturing facility in China.

Simon Mann, a British mercenary, was sentenced to 34 years for plotting a coup in Equatorial Guinea.
Mann claimed he was a junior member, naming Eli Calil as the plot's leader and implicating Mark Thatcher.

Mann previously served jail time in Zimbabwe before extradition and denied being the mastermind.
d70dcffc2c07e08cc121d7126ada53dbc479626e:
UNC student body president Eve Carson was shot multiple times, including in the head, according to her autopsy.

Authorities do not believe Carson was sexually assaulted, despite initial concerns and routine testing.

Anwar Ibrahim claims to have evidence that sodomy charges against him were fabricated by senior government officials.
He says he also has proof of threats on his life, prompting him to seek refuge at the Turkish embassy.
Anwar rejects all charges as politically motivated attempts to undermine his opposition party's gains.
The ruling National Front Coalition has not responded to these allegations.

FDA orders "black box" warnings on certain antibiotics for risk of tendon ruptures and tendonitis.
Fluoroquinolone drugs like Cipro, Levaquin, and Avelox are affected by the new warning.
Risk of tendon rupture is higher in patients over 60, transplant recipients, and those on steroids.
Consumer group Public Citizen urges further action, including direct warnings to doctors.
19fe2a8e0bef37b495afcc83c2f46b60c56039bf:
Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton appeared together at a rally in Unity, New Hampshire.

Clinton publicly endorsed Obama and pledged to help elect him president.
The event aimed to unite Democratic supporters after a contentious primary season.

Seventeen babies in a Texas hospital's NICU received overdoses of the blood thinner heparin.
One infant died, but it's unclear if the overdose caused the death; most others are stable.
The overdose was traced to a mixing error in the hospital pharmacy.
The incident follows a similar high-profile case involving actor Dennis Quaid's twins.

Gina Gershon demands a retraction from Vanity Fair over rumors linking her to Bill Clinton.
Her lawyers call the article false, defamatory, and threaten possible defamation litigation.
Vanity Fair denies implying any improper relationship and refuses to issue a correction.
Bill Clinton harshly criticizes the article and its author, calling the reporting dishonest and unfair.

Over 14,000 residents evacuated from Paradise, California, as wildfires threaten thousands of homes.
More than 1,780 wildfires have burned over 614,000 acres statewide in recent weeks.

Shelters are at capacity, and many evacuees remain uncertain about the fate of their homes.

Nepal's lawmakers will vote Saturday to choose the country's first president since becoming a republic.

Argentine farmers plan next steps after President Kirchner refuses to repeal a 44% agrarian export tax.

Analysts warn the tax could hurt farmer investment and reduce Argentina's appeal to foreign investors.

Illinois man Edward F. Bachner charged with illegal possession of deadly neurotoxin tetrodotoxin.

He faces up to 10 years in prison if convicted.

California's prison director advises against compassionate release for terminally ill Manson family member Susan Atkins.

Atkins, convicted in the 1969 Sharon Tate murders, has been denied parole 11 times and is California's longest-serving female inmate.

North Korea may be cooperating on nuclear disarmament due to severe economic struggles and international sanctions.

Radovan Karadzic, accused architect of Bosnia's worst war crimes, was arrested after a decade-long manhunt.

Nepal's lawmakers will vote Saturday to select the country's first president since becoming a republic.

President Bush defends attending Beijing Olympics, saying a boycott would insult the Chinese people.

Police arrested Courtney Larrell Lockhart for the murder of Auburn University freshman Lauren Burk.
Burk was found shot near campus; her car was later discovered on fire in a campus parking lot.
Lockhart faces multiple capital murder charges related to kidnapping, robbery, and attempted rape.
A campus-wide memorial service for Burk is scheduled, and donations can be made to her favorite charities.
259cc1db3d4c814dafc02ed071327f65a7b9eaad:
Panic and long lines reported at gas stations across multiple states ahead of Hurricane Ike's landfall.
Some stations imposed limits or ran out of regular unleaded, causing frustration and repeated visits.
Gas prices spiked in some areas, with rumors fueling further panic and hoarding.
Police were called to maintain order at overcrowded stations, and fights reportedly broke out.
3bb1cda1e01cb6e14f6190ced3b1ec3f7ef50de9:
Coalition forces handed over security control of Iraq's Anbar province to Iraqi authorities.
Anbar was once a stronghold of Sunni insurgents and al-Qaeda influence.
U.S. troops will remain in the province to support Iraqi forces as needed.
The transfer marks significant progress and is the first Sunni-dominated province to revert to Iraqi control.
7afbe903f1f7cdc1af509583649babf481d01729:
Robert Rogers lost his wife and four children in a 2003 Kansas flash flood.
He chose to honor their memory by becoming a minister and sharing messages of hope.
Rogers has spoken to over 120,000 people, encouraging them to value family and faith.
He established orphanages worldwide in memory of his family and continues to inspire others to live without regrets.
79d625a3311f4ff11598a3ae4fca63103e84e9a1:
29 members of a Russian doomsday cult, including four children, are barricaded in a cave awaiting the apocalypse.
The cult leader, Father Pyotr Kuznetsov, is in police custody and undergoing psychiatric evaluation.
Authorities are concerned for the group's safety as they threaten suicide if forced out.
Temperatures in the cave are below 54°F, raising concerns for the children's wellbeing.
ea6c4267f17d55f1bd9f0950c31ef59565bdc30b:
Six killed and 13 wounded in South Ossetia after Georgian shelling, officials say.
Georgia and South Ossetia blame each other for the violence, with conflicting reports on who started the attack.
Civilians are being evacuated from the conflict zone amid fears of continued military operations.
Russia urges both sides to seek diplomatic solutions and expresses concern over escalating tensions.
851d02b689bec14607574096bb77b9acf53c15ee:
Zimbabwe's inflation rate has skyrocketed to 11.2 million percent, the highest in the world.
Basic goods like bread have become unaffordable, with prices rising from 200,000 to 1.6 trillion Zimbabwe dollars.
The economic crisis has led to mass unemployment, poverty, and emigration, with many struggling to buy essentials.
Political instability and failed unity talks have worsened the situation, deepening Zimbabwe's economic meltdown.
f2d00c8c396698becc18fd2b41ba595c7a3e1cc6:
A columnist humorously describes her frustration with a mysterious coworker, "The Tinkler," who leaves messes in the office bathroom.  
Colleagues become amateur detectives, speculating about the culprit's identity and motives.  
The situation highlights the quirks and irritations of office life and shared spaces.  
The author ultimately longs for more civility and cleanliness in the workplace.
70c6ca3234f9b1221c8aea7671dd78e1ca9d9139:
The Wildland Firefighter Foundation has provided over $1.5 million in aid to more than 500 families since 1999.  
Founder Vicki Minor offers emergency assistance, emotional support, and advocacy for injured and fallen wildfire fighters' families.  
The foundation also built a monument in Boise, Idaho, to honor and help families process their grief.  
Minor's work ensures families receive immediate help and guidance with benefits after tragic losses.
86eaa90c419d5ed93341248fecd8c25d820f2b82:
The New York Giants' win over the Patriots is hailed as one of the biggest Super Bowl upsets.
David Tyree's remarkable catch and Eli Manning's performance are highlighted as game-changing moments.
Fans from across the country and world share emotional and memorable reactions to the game.
Many call it the best Super Bowl ever, with celebrations and pride especially strong among New Yorkers.
a1841cf61c53937fffc54c0e81d8f7cfcad2f0c2:
Deadly Hindu-Christian riots continue in Orissa, India, with about 20 deaths reported so far.
Violence spread to Karnataka, where 14 churches were attacked and 140 people arrested.
The unrest began after the killing of Hindu leader Laxmananda Saraswati, with blame disputed between Christians and Maoists.
Authorities imposed curfews and deployed forces, as both Christian and Hindu sites were destroyed.
5d221c86fdadca378eeaabc39c881cc74efcff07:
Hillary Clinton's campaign apologized for Bill Clinton's harsh language toward a Vanity Fair article.
The article suggested Bill Clinton's behavior changed after heart surgery and cited anonymous sources about alleged affairs.
Bill Clinton called the reporter a "scumbag" and criticized the use of unnamed sources.
Vanity Fair's Todd Purdum defended his reporting, while Clinton's team called the article unfair and inaccurate.
7a49f5bc1d336309f57076bceba91885ee5368b5:
Prince William begins a two-month attachment with the Royal Navy for military experience.
He will train in sea survival, firefighting, and ship damage repair before joining HMS Iron Duke.
William previously completed attachments with the Royal Air Force and British Army.
The training prepares him for his future role as head of the armed forces as king.
dd46893fd318dce4e209f9a335612659ebf8fd24:
Parma sign 18-year-old Alberto Paloschi from AC Milan in a co-ownership deal.
Paloschi scored on his Milan debut but faces limited playing time after new signings.
Parma were relegated from Serie A last season and seek to strengthen their squad.
Milan's Massimo Oddo may leave for Lyon, losing his spot to Gianluca Zambrotta.
acda8749fba5e9553e9f47cef89ef85eae5ad4e3:
JetBlue flight from Boston to Fort Lauderdale diverted after fight over smoking in bathroom.
Plane landed in Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina; one passenger taken into custody.
Fight involved three related individuals, leaving one person injured and bloody.
Passengers were held for two hours while FBI conducted interviews.
20b11518df6520f552d691d5f6c0d02755d11809:
Army Staff Sgt. Darris J. Dawson and Sgt. Wesley Durbin were killed by another U.S. soldier in Iraq.
The military has provided little information to the families, classifying the deaths as "non-hostile."
Dawson's family is frustrated by the lack of transparency and details from the Army.
Critics allege the military downplays combat-related deaths to minimize public perception of war casualties.
51fe083191bb5568bf6a5fea1610a8e19663d7b2:
New York Governor orders state agencies to recognize same-sex marriages from other legal jurisdictions.
The directive follows a state appeals court ruling requiring recognition of out-of-state same-sex marriages.
Agencies must revise policies to comply with New York's human rights law.
California and Massachusetts are among the states whose same-sex marriages will be recognized.
8af047c2d83a91e8b745adfcaa7c282dfe1030a2:
Hurricane Ike's aftermath leaves Texas residents struggling with destroyed homes and uncertain futures.
Evacuees in Austin and San Antonio face hardships, relying on shelters and donations for basic needs.
Power outages and curfews in Ohio spark frustration and efforts to prevent crime amid storm recovery.
Some families, unable to return home, are relocating permanently to new communities for stability.
dfcacd955a9168b2cf599c57a539dbfce43ea2a9:
Susana Trimarco has spent years searching for her daughter, a victim of human trafficking in Argentina.
Her story inspired the hit Argentine soap opera "Stolen Lives," raising awareness about human trafficking.
Trimarco's foundation has rescued 360 women and children and helped push for Argentina's first anti-trafficking law.
Despite threats, Trimarco continues her activism and educates the public while raising her missing daughter's child.
f2463954bc7215d8a1f98cf0d85f280fd2affa58:
A 6.1 magnitude earthquake struck southern Iceland, causing injuries and significant damage to roads and buildings.
The epicenter was about 31 miles from Reykjavik and 6.2 miles below the surface.
Hospitals and infrastructure near the epicenter were damaged, with some evacuations and road closures reported.
No fatalities have been reported, but emergency crews are responding to the affected areas.
0e7a92a6f06e94cdd613860d4a3f4b92a06a2b43:
An American sailor shot and killed two female sailors before shooting himself at a Bahrain military barracks.
The alleged shooter survived and is in critical condition under heavy security at a military hospital.
The Navy stated the incident was not terror-related and involved only U.S. military personnel.
The motive for the shootings remains unclear, and the base was briefly closed after the incident.
2905a15c5947042e42f4b52dc201d24822af20f1:
Over 100 countries adopted a treaty in Dublin to ban cluster bombs due to their long-term danger to civilians.
The treaty requires immediate destruction of stockpiles and cleanup of contaminated areas within eight years.
Major producers like the US, Russia, China, and Israel did not sign but may face international pressure.
Cluster bombs often leave unexploded bomblets, causing civilian casualties decades after conflicts end.
808a3d788317af05686ee71a71d2fbc5c19267bc:
Indonesia will withdraw from OPEC at the end of the year due to declining oil production.
The country has become a net oil importer, importing more than it exports since 2006.
Indonesia's oil production has dropped 32% since 1996, falling below its OPEC quota.
Rejoining OPEC is possible if Indonesia's oil production recovers in the future.
5a1bec30205b6938392f3119366e27d15b4849e8:
Rear Adm. John Stufflebeem was demoted and fired for lying about a sexual affair with a federal employee.
He falsely claimed to be a widower and had sexual relations with the woman in the White House in 1990.
Investigators found Stufflebeem's testimony inconsistent and convicted him of making false official statements.
The woman, "Jane Doe," was found credible and confirmed the affair lasted about eight months.
7ff39e1220c0f084434786ed145e15c6abe48def:
Two U.S. customs agents at JFK Airport were arrested for helping smuggle drugs and contraband.
Agents allegedly accepted bribes to allow illegal drugs and counterfeit goods through security.
A sting operation recorded meetings discussing smuggling hashish, ecstasy, and other items.
Over 20 people have been indicted, and more than 600 pounds of drugs were seized.
7551503e7e57bd519913b0df90ca1e80d5305b05:
Guatemala and Vietnam have halted international adoptions amid concerns of corruption and child trafficking.
Thousands of U.S. families are left in limbo, with pending adoptions under case-by-case review.
New safeguards, including DNA tests and in-person verification, are being implemented to prevent fraud.
The suspension has caused emotional and financial distress for prospective adoptive parents in the U.S.
c34a0cbf6db61323766fc86b1b89b399ff6b0666:
Three men, including Joran Van der Sloot and the Kalpoe brothers, re-arrested in Natalee Holloway's 2005 disappearance.
New evidence prompted the arrests; details of the evidence have not been disclosed.
Suspects charged with involvement in voluntary manslaughter or causing serious bodily harm resulting in Holloway's death.
Holloway's disappearance in Aruba sparked international attention, but she has never been found.
d07a61368a195444b14bd4b382e0fc3d64890b22:
Free Burma Coalition founder criticizes China and India for not pressuring Myanmar to accept more cyclone aid.
ASEAN also accused of failing to help victims effectively after Cyclone Nargis.
Myanmar's military rulers blamed for prioritizing their own security over disaster response.
Official cyclone death toll exceeds 40,000, with fears it could be much higher.
aa68dee4bc246e5aae9507342f8520a2a301a310:
Xenophobic violence in South Africa has killed over 40 people and displaced about 28,000, mainly targeting immigrants.
Clashes spread from Johannesburg to Cape Town, resulting in deaths, injuries, and mass evacuations of foreign nationals.
Mozambique declared a state of emergency after 10,000 of its citizens fled South Africa due to the violence.
South African President deployed the army to help police, while aid workers provide shelter and supplies to affected foreigners.
87ef15064035db73cb29f5cec2f0442f2c527ef0:
Former Illinois Gov. George Ryan will remain in prison after the Supreme Court refused to hear his appeal.
Ryan and co-defendant Larry Warner were convicted of racketeering and fraud related to state license bribes.
Their lawyers argued the trial was unfair due to juror removal, but appeals courts upheld the convictions.
Ryan, governor from 1999-2003, is also known for halting the death penalty in Illinois.
a577b934e210ef7dce07390711f03db8bb2e005a:
Rebel group MEND claimed responsibility for sabotaging a Shell oil pipeline in Nigeria's Rivers State.
MEND alleged killing 11 government soldiers in a clash after the attack, but the military denied this.
The attacks have reduced Nigeria's oil production by about 10%, impacting global oil prices.
MEND seeks a greater share of oil wealth for the impoverished Niger Delta region.
c9414bf55859cb9ca7e925a4a09b416b6f66446a:
Top authors recommend their favorite summer reads for quick escapes and long weekends.
Suggestions include classics, thrillers, literary fiction, and poetry for every reading mood.
Books are grouped by one-day reads, long weekend reads, summer-long savors, and dip-in-and-out options.
Selections range from "The Great Gatsby" to "One Hundred Years of Solitude" and Emily Dickinson's poetry.
f12cda150b96acfa1499b2afb0a81029545abadf:
Barack Obama received endorsements from former Clinton Labor Secretary Robert Reich and ex-Senators Sam Nunn and David Boren.
Reich, a longtime Clinton friend, praised Obama's policy proposals as stronger than Hillary Clinton's.
Nunn and Boren will advise Obama's campaign on national security issues.
The endorsements come days before the crucial Pennsylvania primary.
7359741b0e60b4402f069963a26c7aa766f45e7c:
Researchers have reprogrammed human skin cells to act like embryonic stem cells, sparking debate over research ethics.
Supporters of President Bush's stem cell policy claim the breakthrough vindicates restrictions on embryonic stem cell research.
Opponents argue that embryonic stem cell research should continue, as it may still offer unique medical benefits.
Scientists emphasize that advances in reprogramming cells were built on prior embryonic stem cell research.
b10c71f49dbeabad23325d625e8e7b8529429607:
Josef Fritzl confessed to imprisoning and raping his daughter for 24 years, fathering seven children with her.
Fritzl vacationed in Thailand while his daughter remained trapped in the cellar beneath his house.
Family members, including Elisabeth and her children, have had an "astonishing" reunion after their rescue.
Authorities confirmed Fritzl's paternity through DNA and found no evidence his wife knew of the crimes.
c5a1e04f5f84d406ffedfc2f312c314d1bc03c46:
Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah pledged cooperation in Lebanon's political process and rejected seeking control over the country.
Michel Sleiman was elected president after a six-month vacancy, following a deal to end Lebanon's political crisis.
The new government will include Hezbollah-led opposition with veto power, ensuring broader representation.
Nasrallah emphasized national unity and healing divisions, while Sleiman was praised as a neutral, unifying figure.
8a5035d3527c4db4357dd1db9fb2712008dccea9:
A Texas appeals court ruled the state had no right to remove FLDS children from their parents.
FLDS parents say their children are suffering and feel betrayed while in state custody.
A CNN legal analyst predicts the children are likely to be returned to their families.
Texas Child Protective Services is considering its next steps and maintains its duty to protect children.
3f33d4cb25cd121619ba99eeb02709b72716d622:
Consumer Reports found that cheaper cars can cost more to own long-term than pricier alternatives.  
Depreciation is the largest ownership cost, followed by fuel, interest, insurance, and maintenance.  
Fuel economy and insurance rates can significantly impact total ownership costs between similar models.  
Hybrids like the Toyota Prius and some BMWs can be less expensive to own over five years due to lower fuel or maintenance costs.
e6f451d6667f142a64818a9d05dbc49115e7cbf9:
Presidential candidates are not "regular folks" but members of the elite, with privileged backgrounds and wealth.
Both Democratic and Republican leaders have attended elite schools and enjoy exclusive benefits, including top-tier healthcare.
Attempts by candidates to appear relatable are superficial and do not reflect their true lifestyles.
Media figures criticizing "elitists" are often wealthy and privileged themselves, perpetuating a misleading narrative.
39622c01625247af942a6439699ff529db1d54d0:
Carmen Salva leads Esperanza de Vida, a youth group cleaning up northern Argentina's mountains every Saturday.
The group collects and recycles trash, teaching children environmental responsibility and the importance of caring for "Pachamama."
Participation has grown to about 150 people, including 80 children, with llamas helping carry collected waste.
Salva believes educating youth will create lasting environmental change in her community for future generations.
02927c88776bcc84fc883435a5037b4c8ca70e83:
Sixty-four measles cases reported in the U.S. this year, the highest in seven years.
All but one case involved unvaccinated individuals; some were too young for vaccination.
Most cases were imported from other countries, especially Europe and Israel.
Fourteen patients were hospitalized, but no deaths have occurred.
3ee31d8987c07ba4ea9423d39c5e333525316958:
Amnesty International's annual report highlights global human rights abuses, focusing on China, the US, and Russia.
China faces criticism for repressing activists, minorities, and free speech, especially ahead of the Olympics.
The US is scrutinized for Guantanamo detentions, the death penalty, and leadership failures on human rights.
Other concerns include violence in Iraq and Afghanistan, political repression in Russia, and worsening conditions in Zimbabwe.
f8ddc6d36f378757d27a244529f8fe11672baa41:
Judge declares mistrial in retrial of six men accused of plotting terrorist acts with al Qaeda.
Jury deliberated 13 days without reaching a verdict; this is the second mistrial in the case.
Defendants, known as the "Liberty City 7," allegedly targeted the Sears Tower and FBI offices.
Unclear if government will pursue a third trial; a seventh defendant was previously acquitted.
4e97228ceef1be54ab9151fc6759017e6a54a18d:
U.S. Border Patrol agent Luis Aguilar was killed by a suspected drug smuggler's vehicle near Yuma, Arizona.
Aguilar was deploying spike strips when struck; the suspects fled back into Mexico.
Authorities from both countries are cooperating to apprehend those responsible.
Aguilar is survived by his wife, two children, and a brother who is also a border patrol agent.
0e649ccabaf0e650e9757ee256c2749d37486ba6:
Rozita Swinton is a person of interest in phone calls that led to the Texas polygamist ranch raid.
Swinton, arrested in Colorado for unrelated false reporting, may have impersonated a 16-year-old abuse victim.
Authorities found evidence in Swinton's home possibly linking her to the Texas case.
The raid resulted in 416 children being removed, and officials are still trying to identify the alleged victim.
9eccfcc231c7c0d65cb0c333cb3d1731a5fe2f3f:
Denzel Washington urges leaders to address the crisis facing America's youth, including high dropout and obesity rates.
He credits Boys & Girls Clubs for changing his life and highlights their positive impact on millions of children.
Washington calls on citizens to volunteer, donate, and advocate for youth-focused policies.
He emphasizes holding elected officials accountable for prioritizing children's well-being and future success.
ddf109b3de6022a4ecd62ee2cd31d2c2ac51035b:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.
Protests followed similar demonstrations in London and Paris, disrupting the torch relay and leading to arrests.
The Olympic flame was kept at an undisclosed location ahead of its only U.S. relay, amid security concerns.
Some IOC members suggest ending the global torch relay early due to ongoing disruptions and public relations issues.
bd92eab23374d95ac4430e2e1a8bf7561c662309:
Jockey Frankie Dettori and chef Marco Pierre White co-founded Frankie's restaurants after struggling to find family-friendly dining.
Their restaurant chain has expanded to London, Shanghai, and Dubai since 2004.
Dettori released "Frankie Dettori's Italian Family Cookbook" featuring his favorite childhood recipe, Aubergine Parmigiana.
The recipe is vegetarian, evokes family memories, and serves eight with a three-hour preparation time.
d84440effc748f9220c7dde068962e803f566bc7:
Middlesbrough have released Fabio Rochemback, Gaizka Mendieta, and Dong Gook Lee from their squad.
Manager Gareth Southgate praised Rochemback's contributions and hinted he may return to Sporting Lisbon.
Goalkeeper Mark Schwarzer is in contract talks, with his future at the club uncertain.
Tottenham's Jamie O'Hara signed a new three-year deal, while Radek Cerny joined Queens Park Rangers.
e2866f596c85b0191ce812260d23855c70a01192:
Birmingham City co-owner and managing director were interviewed by police in a corruption probe.
The investigation focuses on payments to a football agent and two players from 2002-03.
No allegations suggest directors or the club benefited financially from the activity.
The club and its directors are fully cooperating with police and resumed share trading after clarification.
4ebd9195bc8c12272b6281a5a610a7b5fb49b8cd:
Jaguars, once thought extinct in the U.S., have been photographed and tracked in Arizona since 1996.
Conservationists fear the U.S.-Mexico border fence will block jaguar migration and threaten their survival.
Environmental groups are challenging Homeland Security's waivers of environmental laws for rapid fence construction.
Wildlife researchers report increased challenges from traffickers and habitat disruption due to border security measures.
4bd37aa7004cd1fdf1ccb506d2159a2cf26995e0:
Sudan and Chad signed a non-aggression pact to halt cross-border hostilities after two days of talks.
The agreement aims to stop support for rebel groups crossing the border and destabilizing each country.
Over 10,000 Darfur refugees recently fled to Chad, joining hundreds of thousands already displaced.
The UN will help ensure both nations honor the agreement, despite past failed peace deals.
29b87dc549b8c64b35517e684e79b1da6316bd88:
International arms dealer Viktor Bout charged with conspiring to sell weapons to Colombia's FARC rebels.
Bout allegedly agreed to provide missiles, rocket launchers, and drones to attack U.S. targets in Colombia.
He faces four terrorism-related charges and is currently in custody in Thailand awaiting possible extradition.
U.S. officials call Bout one of the world's most prolific arms traffickers; his U.S. assets were frozen in 2004.
d2f2fe0b8797b416bae71305c9d1c874e2fdd0cc:
Hillary Clinton's chief strategist, Mark Penn, is stepping down amid controversy over his firm's meeting with Colombian officials.
Penn's firm discussed a U.S.-Colombia trade pact, which Clinton opposes, leading to criticism and his resignation as chief strategist.
Penn will continue to advise Clinton's campaign but will no longer hold his top strategist role.
The Colombian government fired Penn's firm after his remarks, calling them disrespectful.
0269d39abbea3edadd225fa97d818f5a789b4c72:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.
Protests followed similar demonstrations in London and Paris, disrupting the torch relay and leading to arrests.
The IOC will discuss the torch relay amid calls to end the global tour early, but no decision is on the agenda.
China condemned the protests, blaming Tibetan separatists, while some torchbearers withdrew over safety concerns.
394f20c9bee9bc8d0e6a65bac48de57bd9f4733d:
Skybus Airlines is shutting down, becoming the third airline this week to cease operations.
Rising jet fuel costs and a slowing economy were cited as insurmountable challenges for Skybus.
ATA Airlines and Aloha Airlines also announced shutdowns this week amid bankruptcy filings.
Passengers with future Skybus reservations are advised to seek refunds from their credit card companies.
6e63c8b1ae01ab91b4832222edaf4bb461b6c568:
A U.S. sailor was arrested in Japan for the robbery and murder of a Japanese taxi driver.
The suspect, Olatunbosun Ogbogu, was handed over by the U.S. military to Japanese authorities.
Recent crimes involving U.S. service members have fueled controversy and anti-American sentiment in Japan.
U.S. officials pledged cooperation with Japanese authorities to ensure justice is served.
355875856932fd634aaf7c6b26155403aaf15904:
"Street Kings" follows Keanu Reeves as a rule-breaking, troubled LAPD detective entangled in corruption and betrayal.  
The film is a classic Cop Noir, directed by David Ayer and based on a script from James Ellroy.  
Reeves' performance is restrained, while Forest Whitaker energizes as his mentor and boss.  
Despite strong themes, the movie lacks originality and relies on familiar genre tropes.
999523913d281ab57977d79b644f3879ac4d2e37:
Texas authorities are investigating child safety at a polygamist sect's ranch led by Warren Jeffs.
About 400 followers live at the 1,900-acre YFZ Ranch near Eldorado, Texas.
Authorities sealed off the ranch and are interviewing residents, but report no immediate safety issues.
Warren Jeffs, the sect leader, is jailed on charges related to sexual abuse and accomplice to rape.
5f7424604ec11358dc6fdfa47732f90422949041:
Mikhail Gorbachev said Georgia provoked the conflict in South Ossetia, not Russia.
Gorbachev described Georgia's attack on Tskhinvali as a "barbaric assault" using sophisticated weapons.
Georgian President Saakashvili criticized Gorbachev for defending Russia and called his statements "lies and deceptions."
Gorbachev warned that U.S. support for Georgia could harm U.S.-Russia relations.
fdb2b65b9cb99b7d52559ae647cae293cb5910d3:
At least 19 schoolgirls and 2 adults died in a dormitory fire near Kampala, Uganda.
The fire broke out at Buddo Junior School in Mpigi late Monday night.
Survivors reported hearing an explosion before the blaze started.
Conflicting reports emerged about whether the dormitory doors were locked during the fire.
f974fe1b3777ffc5756322c0bb3f31a9b23ec09a:
U.S. officials suspect Pakistan's ISI aided the July 7 bombing of India's Embassy in Kabul.
Evidence includes intercepted communications between Pakistani intelligence and militants, but is not conclusive.
Pakistan strongly denies involvement, calling the allegations unfounded and malicious.
Afghanistan and India have previously accused Pakistan of supporting militants in the region.
2930e1a7ac24bce649bfbf5f5971e74515e38a0a:
Gloria Taylor, mother of murdered schoolboy Damilola Taylor, died of a suspected heart attack in London.
She and her husband founded the Damilola Taylor Trust to help underprivileged youth after their son's death.
Damilola Taylor was killed in 2000, prompting national debate on inner-city issues and youth violence.
Two brothers were convicted of Damilola's manslaughter in 2006 after multiple trials and investigations.
f5f7571a69e96c1d288d939861b7216c2bdc0ae4:
Pope Benedict XVI arrived in the U.S. for a six-day visit, greeted by President Bush at Andrews Air Force Base.
Security is extremely tight, with 27 agencies involved and precautions at major events like Mass at Nationals Park.
The pope aims to energize U.S. Catholics and address issues like the sex abuse scandal and interfaith relations.
Benedict will also visit New York, address the U.N., and celebrate Mass at Yankee Stadium.
5f02aa32bd1dc95e47355755398e31550b232f8a:
Eight Florida teens, ages 14-18, will be tried as adults for a videotaped beating of a 16-year-old.
The attack, allegedly retaliation for online insults, left the victim hospitalized with serious injuries.
Authorities say the teens planned to post the video on YouTube to humiliate the victim.
Experts warn that posting violent "shock videos" online is a growing trend among teens.
9177e5ac94f038749e8d4eb526a65461e0f6df4c:
Judge found Heather Mills exaggerated her wealth and career success before marrying Paul McCartney.
Mills' claim of being McCartney's "business partner" was dismissed as make-belief by the judge.
McCartney was found generous, but did not give Mills 30 valuable paintings as she claimed.
The court valued McCartney's fortune at £400 million, rejecting Mills' higher estimate and her claim of zero earning capacity.
f12e4bbb07211de7d43b4e331dc73404aa804562:
Sarah Knox Taylor Davis, daughter of Zachary Taylor, married Jefferson Davis but died of malaria at 21.
Elizabeth Harrison Walker, Benjamin Harrison's daughter, became a lawyer and economic commentator, active on radio and TV.
Margaret Woodrow Wilson, Woodrow Wilson's daughter, was a singer and activist who later lived in an Indian ashram.
Elizabeth Ann Christian Blaesing, Warren Harding's daughter by his mistress, lived quietly after her mother's tell-all book.
Margaret Truman Daniel,
